description: Mixed, real result: some ontology-evolution vectors regenerate cleanly with zero hand-repair; the exact vector this capability's own falsifier text suggests as an example ('add a new certificate kind') does not, and requires a hand-edit of a pack-source template. This is a genuine falsifier hit, not a tooling limitation, so this capability cannot honestly be marked ALIVE.
prerequisite: This capability could only be evaluated (build+test the regenerated consumer) after applying the frontmatter-strip fix documented in evidence/consumer-replacement.json's FRONTMATTER_LEAK finding, in the scratch copy only.

trials.trial: A: add pcp:ck11 (CertificateKind, rustName "Revocation") -- the literal example the ledger's own falsifier names
trials.ontology_change: pcp:ck11 a pcp:CertificateKind ; pcp:order 11 ; pcp:rustName "Revocation" ; pcp:label "Revocation certificate" .
trials.result: 1 file regenerated (consumer/mfw-pcp-generated/src/certificates.rs), correctly including the new Revocation variant. No generated file was hand-edited.
trials.test_outcome: FAILED: tests/generated_proof.rs::certificate_inventory_is_complete -- assertion `left == right` failed, left: 11, right: 10
trials.root_cause: templates/tests/generated_proof.rs.tmpl line 8 hardcodes the literal Rust source `assert_eq!(certificates::ALL.len(), 10);` -- it is NOT templated from a SPARQL COUNT(?kind) over pcp:CertificateKind, so it never tracks ontology growth. This is an Epistemic Bypass (see .claude/rules/coding-agent-mistakes.md class 2 in the ggen repo): logic that should be derived from RDF is hardcoded inline.
trials.hand_repair_required: Yes -- fixing the now-red test requires hand-editing templates/tests/generated_proof.rs.tmpl (a pack source file) to either compute the count via a SPARQL COUNT query and Tera variable, or drop the hardcoded assertion. The generated .rs file itself needs no manual touch, but the pack cannot regenerate a passing test suite for this ontology class without a human fixing the template.
additional_hardcodes_observed_not_execut: gates/01-certificate-count.rq: 'HAVING (COUNT(?kind) != 10)' -- would also now flag a structural mismatch for the same reason (observed by inspection, not independently run as its own trial)

gates/02-capability-count.rq and templates/README.md.tmpl's prose 'twelve evidence artifacts' carry the analogous hardcode for l5p:Capability (12); a capability-fact addition (the ledger falsifier's other suggested example) was not executed this pass but is expected to hit the same class of defect by inspection of the same pattern

trials.trial: B: add pcp:o03 (ProofObligation) on top of trial A's ontology state
trials.ontology_change: pcp:o03 a pcp:ProofObligation ; pcp:order 3 ; pcp:identifier "MFW-PCP-REVOKE-001" ; pcp:statement "a Revocation certificate strictly postdates the OpenReceipt it revokes" ; pcp:status "UNKNOWN" ; pcp:rustStatus "Unknown" .
trials.result: exactly 1 file regenerated (consumer/mfw-pcp-generated/src/obligations.rs), correctly containing the new obligation entry (id, statement, ObligationStatus::Unknown). No generated file was hand-edited.
trials.test_outcome: Same single pre-existing failure as trial A (certificate_inventory_is_complete); no NEW failure introduced by the obligation addition -- everything referencing obligations (ALL, all_proved()) is fully SPARQL-driven with no hardcoded count anywhere in the templates or gates.
trials.hand_repair_required: No -- this evolution vector is clean.

falsifier_trigger_detail: Trial A (certificate-kind addition, the ledger's own suggested example) requires hand-editing templates/tests/generated_proof.rs.tmpl to keep the generated test suite green.
